why_tempus: Dr. Park is concerned about fusion detection in NSCLC. xR provides whole-transcriptome RNA sequencing to address the limitations of DNA-only testing.
supporting_crm_excerpt: She worries that DNA-only panels may miss relevant fusions and asked what RNA sequencing adds.

product_snapshot.display_text: xR provides whole-transcriptome RNA sequencing for solid tumors and hematologic malignancies.
In a retrospective analysis of patients with identified fusions, incorporating RNA sequencing identified 29% more patients with a unique clinically actionable fusion match than DNA sequencing alone.

objection_response.text: In a retrospective analysis of patients with identified fusions, incorporating RNA sequencing identified 29% more patients with a unique clinically actionable fusion match than DNA sequencing alone. This demonstrates the utility of adding whole-transcriptome RNA sequencing to your existing DNA-only testing workflow. By capturing these events, you can better identify clinically relevant fusions across more than 100 targeted genes.

meeting_script.text: Dr. Park, thank you for sharing your concerns regarding fusion detection in your NSCLC patients. xR provides whole-transcriptome RNA sequencing for solid tumors, which helps address the limitations of DNA-only testing. In a retrospective analysis of patients with identified fusions, incorporating RNA sequencing identified 29% more patients with a unique clinically actionable fusion match than DNA sequencing alone. Would you be open to reviewing how this integration could support your clinical practice for these cases?
